<!-- Generated by the Analysis-tab state-of-the-art review workflow (run wf_7a526974-700), 2026-06-08. Review-only; no application code. -->

# review:bi-pivot-ux

## Strengths to keep

- **Clean engine/UI seam (genuinely above-average for an in-house BI tool).** `runAnalysisQuery` → pure `aggregate(facts, query, registry, options)` → tidy `AnalysisResult`; the React layer never re-aggregates (`PivotTable`, `vizAdapter`, `HeatmapGrid`, `exportUtils`, `DrillPanel` all read the same result). This is exactly the Looker/Mode model (semantic layer + thin presentation) and means almost every improvement below is a UI/query change, not an engine rewrite.
- **Serializable `AnalysisQuery` as the single artifact.** A pivot, a chart, a saved view, and a drill query are all the same object (`src/lib/analysis/types.ts:97`). Presets and saved views are just `Partial<BuilderState>` patches. This is the correct foundation for everything below.
- **Metric registry with base + derived, weighted-avg, movement-scoped `where`** (`metricRegistry.ts`) — a real measure layer, not hardcoded columns. The `Agg` union already supports `sum/avg/min/max/count/distinct/ratio` and `reduceBase` (`aggregate.ts:129`) implements all of them. Most of the "measure control" work is wiring, not engine.
- **`Filter` union already supports `in`/`eq`/`between`** with arity-safe discriminants (`types.ts:58`) and `matchesFilter`/`dimNumber` implement `between` (`aggregate.ts:110-124`). The numeric-between filter is *already in the engine* — only the UI is missing.
- **Correct domain guardrails:** percentage loads excluded from kg sums with a visible note (`unresolvedPctFacts`), planned/performed never conflated, natural sort for `W2 < W10` (`naturalCompare`), dual Y-axis when units differ, the chart "too many series" overload guard. These are signs of a careful author — don't regress them.

## Gaps vs state-of-the-art

### 1. PIVOT TABLE craft (the biggest cluster of gaps)

**1a. No subtotals or grand totals — title: "Pivot with no totals isn't a pivot"**
Why it matters: every BI pivot (Excel, Looker, Metabase) shows a grand-total row and, for nested rows, per-group subtotals. A coach pivoting tonnage by week×movement has no "total per week" and no "grand total" — they must mentally sum columns. This is the single most-expected pivot feature and it is absent.
Concrete change: in `aggregate.ts`, after building `records`, also compute aggregate rows. Two clean options that respect the "engine aggregates, UI renders" invariant: (a) add `subtotalRows`/`grandTotal` to `AnalysisResult` by re-running `computeMeasureValues` over the union of each group's planned/performed facts (correct for `max`/`avg`/`distinct` — you cannot sum a column of maxes), or (b) compute them in the engine keyed by row-prefix. Render in `PivotTable.tsx` as a bold, top-bordered row per row-group boundary plus a final grand-total row. Critical: recompute from facts, never sum the displayed cells (wrong for non-additive aggs and for `adherence`/`delta`).
Files: `src/lib/analysis/aggregate.ts`, `src/lib/analysis/types.ts` (`AnalysisResult`), `src/components/analysis/builder/PivotTable.tsx`, `exportUtils.ts` (totals must export too).
Impact: H · Effort: M

**1b. No sort-by-measure-column — title: "Can't rank by any measure"**
Why it matters: ranking exercises by tonnage, or weeks by adherence, is the most common analytic act. Today rows are locked to `naturalCompare` on the row key (`aggregate.ts:408`). A coach cannot click "Tonnage" to see the biggest contributors first.
Concrete change: add `sort?: { by: 'rowKey' | measureKey; dir: 'asc'|'desc' }` to `BuilderState`/`AnalysisQuery.viz`. Sort `rowKeys` in `aggregate.ts` by the chosen measure value (fall back to `naturalCompare` for ties / null-last). In `PivotTable.tsx` make the leaf `<th>` clickable with an asc/desc caret indicator (lucide `ChevronUp/Down`), defaulting label columns to natural sort. This also unlocks Top-N (1d) — once you can rank, "keep top 10" is trivial.
Files: `builderState.ts`, `types.ts`, `aggregate.ts`, `PivotTable.tsx`.
Impact: H · Effort: M

**1c. No nested-row hierarchy / indent / expand-collapse — title: "Flat tuples instead of an outline"**
Why it matters: with two row dims (e.g. category → exercise) BI tools indent the child under the parent and let you collapse a group; EMOS prints the full `category·exercise` tuple on every row (`rk.map(...)` at `PivotTable.tsx:119`), repeating the category text on each exercise row — high redundancy, low scannability, exactly what the CLAUDE.md density principle warns against.
Concrete change: render only the *changed* leftmost cells per row (classic "outline" suppression of repeated parents), indent child rows, and add a collapse toggle per parent group (collapsed → show only its subtotal from 1a). Pure client-side grouping over the existing ordered `rowKeys`.
Files: `PivotTable.tsx` (+ small `viz.collapsed` set in `BuilderState` if you want it persisted in saved views).
Impact: M · Effort: M

**1d. No in-cell heat-shading within a measure (table view) — title: "Heatmap is a separate viz, not a table affordance"**
Why it matters: `HeatmapGrid.tsx` already proves you have the value-normalisation math (`norm`, the `rgba(24,95,165, …)` ramp). State-of-the-art pivots offer conditional formatting *inside* the table (per-column min→max shading) so a coach scans a normal numeric pivot and still sees where the volume concentrates.
Concrete change: add a per-table "shade cells" toggle (or per-measure). Reuse the `HeatmapGrid` min/max-per-column logic to tint `tdValue` background by the column's value range, keeping the number visible. Respect CLAUDE.md: this is a value/heat colour (data-driven) so it legitimately stays a raw colour, not a token.
Files: `PivotTable.tsx` (extract the `norm`/ramp from `HeatmapGrid.tsx` into a shared `heatShade.ts`).
Impact: M · Effort: S

**1e. No frozen header row / first column — title: "Scroll loses your bearings"**
Why it matters: a 28-week × several-exercise pivot scrolls both axes; once the header and the row-label column scroll away the grid is unreadable. Every serious pivot pins them.
Concrete change: `position: sticky` on `<thead>` cells (`top: 0`) and on the row-label `<td>`/`<th>` (`left: 0`) with a solid `var(--color-bg-primary)` background and a higher `z-index` on the corner. The outer `div` already has `overflow: auto`.
Files: `PivotTable.tsx` (also `HeatmapGrid.tsx`).
Impact: M · Effort: S

**1f. No configurable number format / density — title: "Format is hardcoded per unit"**
Why it matters: `format.ts` hardcodes "kg→t past 10 000", 0–1 decimals, etc. A coach may want raw kg (no t switch), 0 decimals on %1RM, or thousands separators. Density (row height `9px 12px` padding) is fixed; expert tools offer compact/comfortable.
Concrete change: a small "Format" popover — decimals, force-kg-vs-t, show/hide thousands sep — stored on the view; and a compact/comfortable density toggle that swaps the `td` padding. Keep `de()` comma-decimal behaviour (CLAUDE.md).
Files: `format.ts` (parameterise), `builderState.ts`, `PivotTable.tsx`.
Impact: L · Effort: M

### 2. MEASURE control — per-measure aggregation override (clear seam, low effort)

**Title: "Aggregation is locked to the registry default."**
Why it matters: `BuilderState.metrics` is `string[]` (`builderState.ts:31`) and `buildQuery` hardcodes `agg: registry.get(id)?.defaultAgg ?? 'sum'` (`builderState.ts:85`). So `maxLoad` can only ever be `max`, `volume` only `sum`. But the engine *already* takes a per-measure `agg` and `reduceBase` *already* computes avg/min/max/count/distinct. A coach who wants "average weekly tonnage" or "count of distinct exercises" cannot ask for it despite the engine supporting it.
Concrete change: change `metrics: string[]` → `metrics: { id: string; agg?: Agg }[]` (migrate saved views via the existing `version` field), and in the Measures section of `ConfigRail.tsx` add a tiny agg dropdown on each measure chip (sum/avg/min/max/count). `buildQuery` uses the chosen agg, falling back to the default. Show the agg in the pivot leaf header (e.g. "Tonnage (avg)") so the column is self-describing.
Files: `builderState.ts`, `ConfigRail.tsx`, `savedViews.ts` (migration), `PivotTable.tsx`/`vizAdapter.ts` (header label).
Impact: H · Effort: S-M

### 3. FILTERS depth (highest leverage relative to effort)

**3a. Top-N filter — title: "The direct fix for series overload."**
Why it matters: the `ResultChart` "too many series" overload screen (`ResultChart.tsx:176`) tells the coach to "narrow it with a Filter" but provides no tool to do so quickly. Top-N ("top 10 exercises by tonnage") is the canonical one-click fix and a flagship BI feature.
Concrete change: add a `topN?: { dimension: Dimension; measureKey: string; n: number; dir }` to the query. In `aggregate.ts`, after computing per-row values, rank the row-dimension values by the chosen measure and keep the top N (push the rest into an optional "Other" bucket — a nice BI touch). Surface as a control in `ConfigRail` (and a one-click "Keep top 12" button right on the Overload screen). This *and* 1b share the ranking primitive.
Files: `types.ts`, `aggregate.ts`, `ConfigRail.tsx`, `ResultChart.tsx` (CTA).
Impact: H · Effort: M

**3b. Numeric `between` filter — title: "Engine supports it, UI doesn't emit it."**
Why it matters: `between` is fully implemented (`aggregate.ts:120`, `dimNumber` handles `relativeWeek`/`day`/`dayOfWeek`/`intensityZone`/custom) but `ConfigRail` only ever creates `{op:'in'}` filters (`ConfigRail.tsx:195`). A coach cannot say "macro weeks 3–6" or "%1RM 80–90".
Concrete change: when the chosen filter dimension is numeric (the set `dimNumber` recognises), render two number inputs and emit `{op:'between',min,max}` instead of a value-chip list. Add a `FilterRow` variant for it.
Files: `ConfigRail.tsx` only (engine done).
Impact: M · Effort: S

**3c. Search box in long value lists — title: "Picking from 200 exercises by eye."**
Why it matters: `availableValues` is capped at 500 (`aggregate.ts:432`) and `FilterRow` renders every value as a wrap-flow of buttons in a 132px scroll box (`ConfigRail.tsx:405`). For a large exercise library this is unusable.
Concrete change: a text input above the chip list filtering the options client-side; show selected-first. Cheap, big usability win.
Files: `ConfigRail.tsx`.
Impact: M · Effort: S

**3d. Include/exclude toggle — title: "Only 'is one of' is expressible."**
Why it matters: "everything *except* accessories" is a common ask; today you must select all-but-one. BI tools offer is/is-not.
Concrete change: a not-in mode. Either add `{op:'notIn'}` to the `Filter` union + `matchesFilter`, or render an exclude toggle that inverts selection against `availableValues`. The former is cleaner.
Files: `types.ts`, `aggregate.ts`, `ConfigRail.tsx`.
Impact: L-M · Effort: S

**3e. Relative-date scope presets are thin — title: "No 'last completed week', 'this macro to date'."**
Why it matters: scope presets are 4w/8w/12w/YTD/custom (`ConfigRail.tsx:35`). Coaches think in "current mesocycle", "last 4 completed weeks", "since competition". `relativeWeek`/`macro` dimensions exist but there's no scope preset that anchors to them.
Concrete change: add macro-aware scope presets (current macro, current phase) leveraging the existing `Scope.mode:'macro'`. Lower priority than 3a–3c.
Files: `builderState.ts`, `ConfigRail.tsx`, `scopeResolver.ts`.
Impact: L · Effort: M

### 4. DRILL

**4a. Drill target is hardcoded to `exercise` — title: "One drill direction only."**
Why it matters: `onDrill` always sets `rows: ['exercise']` (`AnalysisModule.tsx:96`). Drilling a week×category cell should let the coach choose week→day→session→set, or week→exercise. A single hardcoded target is the weakest part of an otherwise strong drill design.
Concrete change: define an ordered drill hierarchy (`week → date → day → exercise → set`) and, on cell click, drill to the *next* level below the current rows (or offer a small menu: "Break down by …"). The `DrillPanel` already re-runs a real query — only the target dimension selection changes.
Files: `AnalysisModule.tsx` (`onDrill`), small drill-hierarchy constant, `DrillPanel.tsx` (optional breadcrumb).
Impact: M · Effort: M

**4b. No true drill-through to underlying rows — title: "Drill stops at another aggregate."**
Why it matters: BI "drill-through" shows the raw transactional rows behind a cell (here: the individual planned set-lines / performed sets). Today the deepest drill is still an aggregate pivot by exercise. A coach who sees a surprising tonnage cell wants the actual sets (date, load×reps, planned vs performed) that produced it.
Concrete change: the engine currently discards `FactRow[]` after aggregating. Add an opt-in `includeFacts` (or a separate `fetchFactsForCell`) that returns the filtered, un-aggregated facts for the drill query, and render them as a plain detail table in `DrillPanel` (date/exercise/load/reps/state). This is the highest-value drill upgrade for a monitoring tool.
Files: `runAnalysisQuery.ts`/`aggregate.ts` (return filtered facts when asked), `DrillPanel.tsx`.
Impact: M-H · Effort: M

### 5. COMPARISON

**5a. No variance % column in the table — title: "Delta exists, % change doesn't."**
Why it matters: `compare` offers planned/performed/both/Δ/adherence, but adherence is performed÷planned (a level), not period-over-period % change. The ghost overlay (`comparePrevious`) is chart-only — there is no "this period vs last, +12%" in the table where coaches actually read numbers.
Concrete change: extend the period-over-period machinery (which already computes `compareResult` in `AnalysisModule.tsx:81`) into the table: optional "Δ% vs prev period" leaf columns, coloured via the existing `deltaColor`. You already fetch the compare result; it's currently dropped for tables (`state.vizType !== 'table'` guard at line 85).
Files: `AnalysisModule.tsx`, `PivotTable.tsx` (accept an optional compare result), `vizAdapter`/`format` for the % formatting.
Impact: M · Effort: M

**5b. In-cell sparklines / trend arrows — title: "No micro-trend in the table."**
Why it matters: top-tier pivots (and coach dashboards) show a tiny per-row sparkline or an up/down arrow so a 28-week tonnage row reads as a shape, not 28 numbers. The `MonitoringView` tiles already use tone colours — extend that vocabulary.
Concrete change: when a time dimension is on columns, render a small inline SVG sparkline in a leading/trailing cell per row (no new dep — hand-rolled `<path>` like `HeatmapGrid` hand-rolls its grid), or a simple ▲/▼ vs the prior column. Lower priority than 5a.
Files: `PivotTable.tsx` (+ tiny `Sparkline.tsx`).
Impact: M · Effort: M

### 6. EXPORT

**6a. XLSX export — title: "`xlsx` is already a dependency; only CSV is wired."**
Why it matters: `package.json` has `xlsx ^0.18.5` but `exportUtils.ts` only emits CSV/SVG/print. Coaches live in Excel; a real `.xlsx` (with the column-tuple header structure, number formats, and totals from 1a) is expected. CSV loses the grouped header and forces re-formatting.
Concrete change: add `resultToXlsx(result)` using the present `xlsx` lib — reuse the `leaves`/header logic already in `resultToCsv` (`exportUtils.ts:22`), write numbers as numbers (not rounded strings) with a `de`-style number format, and add an "Excel (.xlsx)" item to the export menu.
Files: `exportUtils.ts`, `AnalysisModule.tsx` (menu item).
Impact: M · Effort: S

**6b. Copy-to-clipboard — title: "No paste-into-Excel/email."**
Why it matters: the fastest path coaches use is select→paste. There's no copy action.
Concrete change: a "Copy table" item writing TSV to `navigator.clipboard` (TSV pastes cleanly into Excel/Sheets). Reuse `resultToCsv` with a `\t` delimiter.
Files: `exportUtils.ts`, `AnalysisModule.tsx`.
Impact: M · Effort: S

**6c. PNG export — title: "SVG only; coaches want a paste-able image."**
Why it matters: `exportChartSvg` gives an SVG, but coaches paste PNGs into reports/WhatsApp. SVG won't paste as an image into most tools.
Concrete change: rasterise the cloned SVG to a canvas → `toBlob('image/png')` → download. Pure browser APIs, no dep.
Files: `exportUtils.ts`, `AnalysisModule.tsx`.
Impact: L-M · Effort: S

### 7. PRESETS / saved views

**7a. Saved views are bare (no default, description, last-run, restore) — title: "Views are a flat name list."**
Why it matters: `SavedView` is `{id,name,version,state}` (`savedViews.ts:11`) and the Open dropdown is just a `<select>`. State-of-the-art: pin a default view that loads on open, show a description and last-run timestamp, and protect against accidental overwrite (current `saveView` silently overwrites by slug). There's no "restore to preset defaults" either.
Concrete change: extend `SavedView` with `description?`, `createdAt`, `lastRunAt?`, `isDefault?`; on module mount load the default view if set; warn before overwrite; show description as the `<option title>` (you already do this for presets at `AnalysisModule.tsx:202`). The `version` field is already there for migration.
Files: `savedViews.ts`, `SaveViewModal`, `AnalysisModule.tsx`.
Impact: M · Effort: S-M

**7b. No "reset to default state" / clear — title: "Easy to get lost, hard to get back."**
Why it matters: after stacking dims/filters there's no one-click return to `defaultBuilderState`.
Concrete change: a small "Reset" action in the toolbar calling `setState(defaultBuilderState(today, …))`.
Files: `AnalysisModule.tsx`.
Impact: L · Effort: S

### 8. Other coach-grade analytics features

**8a. No "Totals/% of total" measure transform — title: "Can't express share-of."**
Why it matters: "category X is 23% of weekly tonnage" needs % of column/grand-total, a standard pivot calc. Today `pullPctOfTotal` is a bespoke derived metric; it doesn't generalise. A generic "% of row / % of column / % of total" toggle (computed from the totals in 1a) would cover many coach questions without new metrics.
Files: `aggregate.ts` (needs totals from 1a first), `PivotTable.tsx`, `ConfigRail.tsx`.
Impact: M · Effort: M (depends on 1a)

**8b. No swap-rows/columns ("pivot") action — title: "Transpose requires manual re-chipping."**
Why it matters: flipping rows↔cols is a one-click staple of every pivot tool; here you must remove and re-add chips.
Concrete change: a swap-arrows button between the Rows and Columns sections that swaps `state.rows`/`state.cols`.
Files: `ConfigRail.tsx`.
Impact: L · Effort: S

**8c. Drill panel can't itself drill / export — title: "Dead-end side panel."**
Why it matters: `DrillPanel` renders a `PivotTable` *without* the `onDrill` prop (`DrillPanel.tsx:26`), so the drill is one level deep and not exportable. With 4a/4b this becomes a navigable detail surface.
Files: `DrillPanel.tsx`.
Impact: L-M · Effort: S

**8d. Empty/overload states are good; add a "why is this empty?" affordance.** The Empty states are clear, but when filters exclude everything there's no hint which filter is responsible. Minor: show active filter count + a "clear filters" link in the empty state.
Files: `PivotTable.tsx`, `ConfigRail.tsx`.
Impact: L · Effort: S

## Open questions

- **Totals for non-additive aggregates:** for `max`/`avg`/`distinct`/`adherence`/`delta`, a column "total" must be recomputed from facts, not summed from cells. Confirm the engine should expose subtotal/grand-total records (preferred, keeps the invariant) rather than the UI summing — I've assumed yes.
- **Saved-view storage:** views are `localStorage` per-device today (`savedViews.ts`) with the gated `analysis_views` table noted as a later sign-off swap. Should "pin default / last-run" wait for that table, or ship on localStorage now? (I'd ship on localStorage; the shape migrates.)
- **Scope of this work vs CLAUDE.md:** the Analysis module is explicitly **out of scope for the current review** ("disable in UI, keep code intact"). These are strong improvements but should not be implemented until Analysis is brought back in scope — this review is a forward-looking backlog, not an implement-now plan. Worth confirming the intent is to queue these.
- **Top-N "Other" bucket:** do coaches want the remainder rolled into an "Other" row (keeps totals honest) or simply dropped? Affects 3a design.
- **Drill-through volume:** returning raw `FactRow[]` for a cell could be large for a group over 12 weeks. Cap + "showing first N" like the existing `availableValues` 500-cap?
